summary: Design a neuro-symbolic memory architecture for autonomous agricultural agents, inspired by the brain's Complementary Learning Systems (CLS). The architecture decouples memory into a fast-learning episodic Knowledge Graph for temporal/spatial sequences (crop cycles, disease spread) and a slow-learning semantic ML layer that compresses mature data into generalized patterns, with an agent controller orchestrating multi-layer reasoning.
assumptions: Sufficient structured agricultural data (e.g., crop rotation logs, disease progression timelines, spatial field maps) exists to populate the episodic Knowledge Graph layer
The agent controller can effectively route queries across the episodic KG and semantic ML layers based on query type (temporal/spatial vs. generalized pattern matching)
The semantic ML layer will be updated via periodic offline training on consolidated episodic patterns rather than online streaming
Agricultural domain experts can provide the ontology schema for the Knowledge Graph (entities, relations, temporal annotations)
